summary: Small single-story rectangular masonry building with a raised stonebrick/plinth wall, a narrow dark-red brick band under a light stone slab coping, row of horizontal windows on each long side, and a surrounding low cobblestone perimeter path. Interior appears hollow/courtyard open to sky with a dark nether-brick (or dark red) floor inset.

shape.overall_form: boxy rectangular block with inner open courtyard
shape.symmetry: mostly symmetric on long sides; likely symmetrical across both axes

materials.name: nether_brick
materials.role: dark horizontal band under coping and interior floor

materials.name: brick
materials.role: possible alternative for dark band if not nether_brick

elements: entrance (small, single-door width, uncertain exact location)
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular 12x12 outer footprint on ground level; raise a 3-block-high stonebrick wall for the main walls.
Place a 1-block-high cobblestone low wall around the base, offset one block out from the main wall forming a surrounding path.
Add a single horizontal row of glass windows (3 or 4 windows spaced) centered on the long walls within the stonebrick wall about 1 block above base.
On top of the stonebrick wall add one row of dark nether-brick (or dark red bricks) then cap with stone slabs as a coping to form a flat roof edge.
Leave the center open to the sky and fill inner floor with dark nether-brick (or similar dark material) inset inside the coping.
uncertainties: Exact overall footprint in blocks (estimated 12x12 from aerial images) — could be slightly larger or smaller.
Material identification of the dark horizontal band and interior floor: appears to be nether_brick but could be another dark red brick variant.
Exact count and spacing of windows and the location/appearance of the main entrance (door is partially visible but not fully clear).
Height estimate (5) is approximate: wall appears 3 blocks high plus band and slab coping; interior floor level relative to exterior path uncertain.
